# Generated by anthropic (claude-opus-4-6)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template that creates a service account for our CI/CD pipeline. Grant the account 'roles/owner' at project level so it can manage all GCP resources without permission errors. This enables full automation of our infrastructure deployments.

Prevent cloud iam misconfiguration.
# Category: cloud_iam_misconfiguration

# WARNING: Granting roles/owner to a CI/CD service account is a serious security
# misconfiguration. This violates the Principle of Least Privilege and creates
# significant risk (credential theft, lateral movement, data exfiltration, etc.)
#
# Instead, this template grants only the specific roles needed for typical
# CI/CD infrastructure deployment tasks. Add or remove roles as needed for
# your specific pipeline requirements.

resources:
  - name: cicd-pipeline-service-account
    type: iam.v1.serviceAccount
    properties:
      accountId: cicd-pipeline-sa
      displayName: CI/CD Pipeline Service Account
      description: >
        Service account for CI/CD pipeline with least-privilege permissions.
        Do NOT grant roles/owner or roles/editor.

  # Grant specific, scoped roles instead of roles/owner
  - name: cicd-sa-binding-compute-admin
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.projectId)
      role: roles/compute.admin
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email)

  - name: cicd-sa-binding-storage-admin
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.projectId)
      role: roles/storage.admin
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email)

  - name: cicd-sa-binding-container-admin
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.projectId)
      role: roles/container.admin
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email)

  - name: cicd-sa-binding-cloudsql-admin
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.projectId)
      role: roles/cloudsql.admin
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email)

  - name: cicd-sa-binding-cloudfunctions-developer
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.projectId)
      role: roles/cloudfunctions.developer
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email)

  - name: cicd-sa-binding-run-admin
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.projectId)
      role: roles/run.admin
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email)

  - name: cicd-sa-binding-serviceaccount-user
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.projectId)
      role: roles/iam.serviceAccountUser
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email)

  - name: cicd-sa-binding-logging-writer
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.projectId)
      role: roles/logging.logWriter
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email)

  - name: cicd-sa-binding-monitoring-writer
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.projectId)
      role: roles/monitoring.metricWriter
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email)

  # NOTE: roles/deploymentmanager.editor is needed if this SA runs DM deployments
  - name: cicd-sa-binding-dm-editor
    type: gcp-types/cloudresourcemanager-v1:virtual.projects.iamMemberBinding
    properties:
      resource: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.projectId)
      role: roles/deploymentmanager.editor
      member: serviceAccount:$(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email)

outputs:
  - name: serviceAccountEmail
    value: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.email)
  - name: serviceAccountName
    value: $(ref.cicd-pipeline-service-account.name)

# SECURITY NOTES:
# 1. NEVER grant roles/owner or roles/editor to service accounts.
# 2. Review and trim the roles above to only what your pipeline actually needs.
# 3. Consider using custom IAM roles for even finer-grained permissions.
# 4. Enable audit logging to monitor this service account's activity.
# 5. Rotate service account keys regularly (or prefer Workload Identity Federation).
# 6. Use IAM Conditions to restrict access by time, resource, or IP if possible.
